question.text: In a compulsory delivery commodity futures contract like Gold, what happens to all open positions on the maturity date?

answer.text: All outstanding positions result in physical delivery of the underlying commodity
All positions are cash-settled at the settlement price
Positions are automatically rolled over to the next month
Only seller's positions result in delivery

explanation.text: **The Logic:** In compulsory delivery contracts, ALL outstanding positions on maturity result in physical delivery. Both buyer and seller must fulfill delivery obligations. **The Trap:** Not cash-settled (that's a different settlement type). Not rolled over automatically. BOTH buyer and seller positions result in delivery. **Cross-Exam Context:** Compulsory delivery is specific to commodity futures. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Speculators must exit compulsory delivery contracts well before the tender period — failure to do so creates delivery obligations they may not be able to fulfill.
